segments.text: What does the computer industry thrive on apart from anarchy?

translations.zh-Hans: 除了无序状态，计算机产业还依靠什么蓬勃发展？

segments.text: trends may push Silicon Valley back to the future.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些趋势也许会把硅谷推回未来。

segments.text: Carver Mead, a pioneer in integrated circuits and a professor of computer science at the California Institute of Technology, notes there are now workstations that enable engineers to design, test,

translations.zh-Hans: 集成电路先驱、加州理工学院计算机科学教授卡弗·米德指出，如今已有工作站使工程师能够直接在桌面上设计、测试

segments.text: and produce chips right on their desks, much the way an editor creates a newsletter on a Macintosh.

translations.zh-Hans: 并生产芯片，就像编辑在麦金塔电脑上制作简报一样。

segments.text: As the time and cost of making a chip drop to a few days and a few hundred dollars, engineers may soon be free to let their imaginations soar without being penalized by expensive failures.

translations.zh-Hans: 随着制造芯片的时间和成本降至几天和几百美元，工程师很快便可让想象力自由驰骋，而不必因代价高昂的失败受罚。

segments.text: predicts that inventors will be able to perfect powerful customized chips over a weekend at the office, spawning a new generation of garage startups and giving the U.S.

translations.zh-Hans: 米德预言，发明者将能在办公室利用一个周末完善强大的定制芯片，从而催生新一代车库创业公司，并让美国

segments.text: a jump on its foreign rivals in getting new products to market fast.

translations.zh-Hans: 在迅速把新产品推向市场方面领先国外竞争对手。

segments.text: We've got more garages with smart people, Mead observes.

translations.zh-Hans: 米德说：‘我们有更多车库，也有更多聪明人。’
‘我们确实在无序中蓬勃发展。’

segments.text: Already, Orientals and Asian Americans constitute the majority of the engineering staffs at many valley firms, and Chinese, Korean, Filipino, and Indian engineers are graduating in droves from California's

translations.zh-Hans: 在许多硅谷企业，东方人和亚裔美国人已经构成工程人员的多数；大批华裔、韩裔、菲律宾裔和印度裔工程师正从加州的

segments.text: As the heads of next-generation startups, these Asian innovators can draw on customs and languages to forge tighter links with crucial Pacific Rim markets.

translations.zh-Hans: 作为下一代初创企业的负责人，这些亚洲创新者能利用自己的风俗和语言，与至关重要的环太平洋市场建立更紧密的联系。

segments.text: For instance, Alex Aul, a Stanford PhD from Hong Kong, has set up a Taiwan factory to challenge Japan's near-lock on the memory chip market.

translations.zh-Hans: 例如，来自香港、拥有斯坦福博士学位的亚历克斯·奥尔已在台湾设厂，挑战日本对存储芯片市场的近乎垄断。

segments.text: Damodar Reddy's tiny California company reopened an AT&T chip plant in Kansas City last spring with financing from the state of Missouri.

translations.zh-Hans: 达莫达尔·雷迪所创办的加州小公司，去年春天借助密苏里州的资助，重新启用了美国电话电报公司在堪萨斯城的一家芯片厂。

segments.text: Before it becomes a retirement village, Silicon Valley may prove a classroom for building a global business.

translations.zh-Hans: 在变成退休村之前，硅谷也许会成为建设全球化企业的课堂。
